text:
Olympicsjudo star Lubjana Piovesana revelled in revenge after defeating a Team GB athlete who previously “attacked” her during a training session. Piovesana quit Team GB in July 2021 after accusing Lucy Renshall and coach Jamie Johnson of bullying and now represents Austria.

Piovesana and Renshall went head-to-headon the judo floor in the second round of the women’s -63kg finals at the Champ-de-Mars Arena.

And the contest was decided by a golden score, judo’s equivalent of sudden death, after the judges were unable to separate them. Piovesana eventually secured the victory after flipping Renshall onto her back.

According toThe Times, Piovesana had previously accused Renshall of a physical assault, which resulted in a police report and a two-year investigation into a “toxic” culture at Team GB’s training centre in Walsall.

And following her Olympics 2024 victory, Piovesana explained: “People thought we were going to punch each other. She was one of the reasons I left British Judo. She was the one who attacked me, so it was quite important to win today.

“I never thought in a million years I was going to be here at the Olympics [after what happened at British Judo], so I’m happy just to be here. I’m happy I won. I feel like I made it.”

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Piovesana, who speaks with a Birmingham accent, was raised in Sutton Coldfield in a family of judo enthusiasts. She was funded by UK Sport after joining the British judo senior team as a 17-year-old and is the youngest Briton to win a medal on the top-tier IJF World Tour.

But the now 27-year-old later alleged that she was “attacked” by Renshall while lying on a mat during a conditioning session in July 2019. The incident was reported to West Midlands police.

“Little things started happening, little comments all the time, it was getting upsetting, and then she attacked me,” Piovesana said. “The coach was quite cruel to me, making comments. My dad wrote an email saying, ‘My daughter is really upset, please can something be done?’ And he read it out on the mat in front of everyone and then he wouldn’t talk to me. I felt like I was being victimised.”

Don't miss:Olympics LIVE: Simone Biles shares savage team name as boxer faces backlash

Don't miss:Team GB pole vaulter set for £10million payday thanks to Olympics and modelling

An independent investigation was launched after the incident which looked into bullying within British Judo and included allegations from other athletes.

But in April 2021, specific accusations of bullying were rejected by the adjudication panel, which made a “number of recommendations” and identified “areas of development” for British Judo.

By that stage Piovesana had already moved to Austria with her boyfriend, who is an elite judoka. She was granted an Austrian passport last year after the Austrian Judo Federation supported her application. And she went on to qualify for a place at the Paris Olympics.

British Judo, meanwhile, are believed to consider the matter closed following the findings of the investigation and have previously declined to comment on it.

title: Olympics judo star revels in revenge after quitting Team GB to represent Austria | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Parsons
description: An Olympic judo star has issued her say after defeating an opponent who previously ‘attacked' her.
